Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned D180 (204DTD) engine typically costs £2,500–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Main dealer new engines can exceed £6,500 supply only. The chain job itself can cost £1,500–£3,500.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is one of the more problematic Ingenium diesel variants. The 204DTD has a 'remarkably long list of issues' including timing chain failure (as low as 40,000–60,000 miles), turbo failure, injector over-fuelling, and DPF clogging. Specialist rebuilders report seeing these engines regularly with catastrophic failures. It was a short-run engine for good reason.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch and failure (the most common and costly issue, often an engine-out job), turbocharger failure (which can cause hydro-lock and bent conrods), fuel injector over-fuelling (which can melt pistons), DPF clogging (accelerated by urban driving), and oil dilution from DPF regeneration.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an Evoque D180 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold: if your Evoque is worth £15,000 and a recon engine is £4,000 (27%), it's worth it. If the vehicle is a tired £8,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. These are valuable vehicles that often justify replacement spend, but the short-run nature of the D180 may make parts harder to source.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 2019–2020 D180 uses the 204DTD (AJ20D4) — a 2.0-litre twin-turbo Ingenium diesel producing 180 PS. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap requiring a full engine replacement. It's an engine-out job, so labour costs are high. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the 204DTD. See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an Evoque D180 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The tightly packed engine bay and twin-turbo system add complexity to the job.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Evoque D180 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling or metallic noise on cold start, engine warning light, rough idling, loss of power, "engine fault" message.
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — can fail as low as 60,000 miles.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: at first sign of rattle, book inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The timing chain is the most critical weak point on this engine. The components are prone to failure because of the weak materials used, and plastic particles from worn guides can block the oil pickup, causing spun bearings. It's an engine-out job, so labour costs are high. Regular oil changes (every 8,000 miles, not the suggested 21,000–24,000) are critical. If you hear any rattle on startup, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, high-pitched whining noise, excessive black smoke under load, sluggish acceleration.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; turbo failure can cause catastrophic engine damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The twin-turbo system adds complexity and failure points. Turbocharger components on diesel Ingenium engines are "seemingly not sufficient for their purpose." A failed turbo can cause engine failure due to the intake of oil and debris, and excessive oil passing through the intake can hydro-lock the engine, bending conrods. If you notice any whistling, smoke, or power loss, have the turbo inspected immediately.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Over-fuelling & DPF Clogging
problems.symptoms: Black smoke, rough idle, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, DPF warning.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The fuel injectors can over-fuel, causing excessive heat that can melt pistons made of "weaker than expected material." DPF clogging is accelerated by short urban journeys. Regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regeneration are essential. If you see black smoke or a DPF warning, have the system inspected promptly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Evoque D180 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Replace turbo before it causes hydro-lock damage
Forced regeneration or clean; require motorway driving
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This D180? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes every 8,000 miles or less
* Timing chain replaced as preventative maintenance (especially at 60k+ miles)
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle or ticking — indicates timing chain issues
* Whistling, smoke, or power loss — turbo or injector issues
* Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Primarily urban driving history — DPF clogging is almost guaranteed

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The D180 is a short-run, high-power diesel that is "a lot of trouble" according to specialists. With its well-documented timing chain, turbo, and injector vulnerabilities, buying one without a documented service history is a significant risk. A £4,000 reconditioned engine on an Evoque valued at £15,000 is 27% of the car's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. On a sub-£8,000 example, think carefully. The 60% Land Rover scrap threshold applies.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Evoque D180 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Evoque D180 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic noise on startup (timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including oil changes every 8,000 miles (not the suggested 21,000–24,000)
* Turbo Check — Test acceleration; any whistling, smoke, or power loss?
* Smoke Check — Black smoke? (injector over-fuelling or DPF) / Blue smoke? (turbo oil leak)
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate clogging
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* Oil Level — Check for diesel contamination (oil dilution from DPF regen)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
